title: Tech Club Members
description: You are given three tables: clubs with categories, students, and memberships. Find distinct names of students who are members of at least one 'Tech' category club, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
clubs(id, name, category)
students(id, name, year)
memberships(id, student_id, club_id, joined_year)
outputFormat: One student name per line, ordered alphabetically. No duplicates.
constraints: Use two JOINs and DISTINCT. Filter WHERE category = 'Tech'. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT DISTINCT s.name FROM students s JOIN memberships m ON s.id = m.student_id JOIN clubs c ON m.club_id = c.id WHERE c.category = 'Tech' ORDER BY s.name;
hint: Join students → memberships → clubs, filter by category = 'Tech', use DISTINCT.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE clubs (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category TEXT); CREATE TABLE students (id INTEGER, name TEXT, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE memberships (id INTEGER, student_id INTEGER, club_id INTEGER, joined_year INTEGER); INSERT INTO clubs VALUES (1,'Coding Club','Tech'),(2,'Chess Club','Games'),(3,'Robotics','Tech'); INSERT INTO students VALUES (1,'Alice',2),(2,'Bob',1),(3,'Charlie',3),(4,'Diana',2); INSERT INTO memberships VALUES (1,1,1,2022),(2,2,2,2021),(3,3,1,2023),(4,3,3,2022),(5,4,2,2022);
